pages.raw_text:
The woman suffrage victory in
Washington last Tuesday was cele-
-brated last evening by Spokane's
most prominent suffragets with a
banquet in the chamber of COM
merce banquet room, and felicia
-tions over the successful outcome
of the campaign were in order on
all sides.


Mrs. May Arkwright Hutton, who
has been one of the leaders in the
cause, bestowed eloquent praise
upon Helen LaReine Baker, who as
sisted rhe in the fight for suffrage
In Olympia two years ago.


Mrs. Rose Moore who made ring
ing campaign speeches from the
Idaho line to the coast. was intro
duced by Toastmistress Mrs. &Min-
ME J. Reynolds as the best little
campaigner in the state" and deliv-
cred short talk which won much
applause.


Mrs. P. P. Stafford, who accom-
panied Mrs. Moore on her campaign
trip, responded to Echoes From
the Campaign" and told of expert
ences in speaking in a saloon in
Georgetown


- Mrs. A. E. House, president of the
-Woman's club, responded to the
toast, THE Clubwoman," declaring
that every member of her club was
also member of the Political
Equality league.


= A most enjoyable toast was that
